content:
Inference familyHow to make predictionsSuitable training setups
Autoregressive decoding (Vacareanu et al., 2024)Parse from string: hatyARD(x) = float(arg max y∈Y p(str(y) | x))PEFT/SFT: Minimize token-level CE on str(y*). LCE = - ∑t=1|str(y*)| log p(yt | x, y<t)
Regression-aware inference (RAIL) (Lukasik et al., 2024)Bayes-optimal from p(· | x): hatyRAIL(x) = arg minv∈R Eyy~p[ℓ(float(y), v)] When ℓ = Lmse, same as posterior mean: hatyRAIL(x) ≈ ∑y∈Ygrid y · p(str(y) | x)PEFT/SFT: (i) Minimize LCE. (ii) RAFT (Lukasik et al., 2025). LCRAFT = (y* - Eyy~p[float(y)])^2
Predictive Head (Tang et al., 2025)Extract representations, then regress: hatyHEAD(x) = fREG(φ(H(x)))Head-only: train fREG with Lmse. PEFT/SFT + head: fine-tune back-bone jointly with fREG.
RELISH (ours)Iteratively refine a latent state, then regress: r(i) = Refine(r(i-1), H(x)), i = 1,..., L hatyRELISH(x) = w^T r(L) + bHead-only: train r(0), w, b, and refinement modules with Lmse. PEFT/SFT + head: optionally fine-tune backbone jointly with REL-ISH modules.

MethodFamilyTrainingInference
Zero / Many Shot (Vacareanu et al., 2024)AutoregressiveNoneSingle pass
Linear / MLP (Tang et al., 2025)Predictive headLowSingle pass
RAIL (Lukasik et al., 2024)Regression-awareNoneMulti-sample
RAFT (Lukasik et al., 2025)Regression-awareHighMulti-sample
RELISH (ours)Predictive headLowSingle pass

\[
\hat{y}_{\text{ARD}}(x) = \text{float} (\arg \max_{y\in \mathcal{Y}}p(\text{str}(y)\mid x))
\]

\[
\hat {y} _ {\text {R A I L}} (x) = \arg \min  _ {v \in \mathbb {R}} \mathbb {E} _ {y \sim p (\cdot | x)} [ \ell (\text {f l o a t} (y), v) ].
\]

\[
\hat {y} _ {\text {H E A D}} (x) = f _ {\text {R E G}} \left(\phi (H (x))\right).
\]

\[
\mathcal {L} _ {\mathrm {C E}} = - \sum_ {t = 1} ^ {| s t r (y ^ {*}) |} \log p \left(y _ {t} \mid x, y _ {<   t}\right).
\]

\[
\mathcal {L} _ {\mathrm {R A F T}} = \left(y ^ {*} - \mathbb {E} _ {y \sim p} [ \text {f l o a t} (y) ]\right) ^ {2},
\]

\[
r ^ {(i)} = \operatorname {R e f i n e} \left(r ^ {(i - 1)}, H (x)\right), \qquad i = 1, \dots , L,
\]

\[
\hat {y} _ {\text {R E L I S H}} (x) = w ^ {\top} r ^ {(L)} + b.
\]

\[
H (x) = \operatorname {L L M} _ {\text {f r o z e n}} (x) \in \mathbb {R} ^ {S \times d}.
\]

\[
z = \frac {y ^ {*} - \mu_ {y}}{\sigma_ {y} + \epsilon}, \quad \hat {y} = \hat {z} (\sigma_ {y} + \epsilon) + \mu_ {y},
\]

\[
X (x) = H (x) W, \qquad W \in \mathbb {R} ^ {d \times d _ {h}},
\]

\[
\tilde {r} ^ {(i)} = L N \big (r ^ {(i - 1)} + \mathrm {M H A} \big (r ^ {(i - 1)}, X (x), X (x) \big) \big), \qquad r ^ {(i)} = L N \big (\tilde {r} ^ {(i)} + \mathrm {F F N} \big (\tilde {r} ^ {(i)} \big) \big).
\]

\[
\operatorname {A t t n} _ {m} \left(Q _ {m}, K _ {m}, V _ {m}\right) = \operatorname {s o f t m a x} \left(\frac {Q _ {m} K _ {m} ^ {\top}}{\sqrt {d _ {h} / M}}\right) V _ {m}.
\]

\[
\mathcal {L} _ {\mathrm {H u b e r}} (\hat {z}, z) = \left\{ \begin{array}{l l} \frac {1}{2} (\hat {z} - z) ^ {2}, & | \hat {z} - z | \leq \delta , \\ \delta \big (| \hat {z} - z | - \frac {1}{2} \delta \big), & \text {o t h e r w i s e}, \end{array} \right.
\]

Dataset: STS-B
System PromptYou are an expert human annotator for semantic textual similarity (STS). You will see English sentence pairs and judge how similar in MEANING they are.
Scoring instructions: - Output a real-valued similarity score from 0.000 to 5.000, inclusive. - 5.000 = same meaning / paraphrases. - 2.500 = partially similar with important differences. - 0.000 = completely unrelated meaning.
CRITICAL FORMAT RULE: Return ONLY the numeric score, with EXACTLY three digits after the decimal point (e.g., 3.000). Do NOT include any words, punctuation, units, labels, or explanation.
User MessageSentence A: {sentence_a} Sentence B: {sentence_b}
Score (0.000 to 5.000 only):

Dataset: SICKR-STS
System PromptYou are an expert annotator for semantic textual similarity (STS) for Sentences Involving Compositional Knowledge. You will see sentence pairs and judge how similar in MEANING they are.
Scoring instructions: - Output a real-valued similarity score from 1.000 to 5.000, inclusive. - 5.000 = same meaning / paraphrases; 1.000 = completely unrelated.
CRITICAL FORMAT RULE: Return ONLY the numeric score, with EXACTLY three digits after the decimal point (e.g., 3.000). Do NOT include any words, punctuation, units, labels, or explanation.
User MessageSentence A: {sentence_a} Sentence B: {sentence_b}
Score (1.000 to 5.000 only):

Dataset: WMT_EN_ZH
System PromptYou are an expert human annotator for translation quality / semantic textual similarity (STS). You will see sentence pairs where Sentence A is in English (source) and Sentence B is in Chinese (translation). You must judge how well the meaning is preserved from English to Chinese.
Scoring instructions: - Output a real-valued translation quality / similarity score from 0.000 to 100.000. - 100.000 = perfect meaning preservation (excellent translation / paraphrases). - 50.000 = partially similar meaning but with important differences or missing info. - 0.000 = completely unrelated or very bad translation.
CRITICAL FORMAT RULE: Return ONLY the numeric score with EXACTLY three digits after the decimal point. Do NOT include any words, punctuation, units, labels, or explanation.
User MessageSentence A (English source): {sentence_a} Sentence B (Chinese translation): {sentence_b} Score (0.000 to 100.000 only):

Dataset: WMT_RU_EN
System PromptYou are an expert human annotator for translation quality / semantic textual similarity (STS). You will see sentence pairs where Sentence A is in Russian (source) and Sentence B is in English (translation). You must judge how well the meaning is preserved from Russian to English.
Scoring instructions: - Output a real-valued translation quality / similarity score from 0.000 to 100.000. - 100.000 = perfect meaning preservation (excellent translation / paraphrases). - 50.000 = partially similar meaning but with important differences or missing info. - 0.000 = completely unrelated or very bad translation.
CRITICAL FORMAT RULE: Return ONLY the numeric score with EXACTLY three digits after the decimal point. Do NOT include any words, punctuation, units, labels, or explanation.
User MessageSentence A (Russian source): {sentence_a} Sentence B (English translation): {sentence_b}
Score (0.000 to 100.000 only):

Dataset: WMT_SILEN
System PromptYou are an expert human annotator for translation quality / semantic textual similarity (STS). You will see sentence pairs where Sentence A is in Sinhala (source) and Sentence B is in English (translation). You must judge how well the meaning is preserved from Sinhala to English.
Scoring instructions: - Output a real-valued translation quality / similarity score from 0.000 to 100.000. - 100.000 = perfect meaning preservation (excellent translation / paraphrases). - 50.000 = partially similar meaning but with important differences or missing info. - 0.000 = completely unrelated or very bad translation.
CRITICAL FORMAT RULE: Return ONLY the numeric score with EXACTLY three digits after the decimal point. Do NOT include any words, punctuation, units, labels, or explanation.
User MessageSentence A (Sinhala source): {sentence_a} Sentence B (English translation): {sentence_b} Score (0.000 to 100.000 only):
